title: ReferenceError
body:
## Definition

**ReferenceError** is thrown when code references an [identifier](concept:identifier) that cannot be resolved — undeclared variable, or variable in the [temporal dead zone](concept:temporal-dead-zone).

## Why it exists

Fails fast instead of silently producing wrong results (unlike `var` before declare returning [undefined](concept:undefined)).

## How it works

```js
console.log(notDeclared); // ReferenceError

let x = 1;
{
  console.log(x); // TDZ ReferenceError
  let x = 2;
}
```

## Common confusion

`typeof undeclaredName` returns `"undefined"` without throwing. Accessing a TDZ binding throws even with `typeof` in some cases. Strict mode does not change ReferenceError for TDZ.

## In practice

Debugging: check spelling, import paths, block scope leaks, and hoisting order.
bodyLinked:
## Definition

**[ReferenceError](concept:reference-error)** is thrown when code references an [identifier](concept:identifier) that cannot be resolved — undeclared variable, or variable in the [temporal dead zone](concept:temporal-dead-zone).

## Why it exists

Fails fast instead of silently producing wrong results (unlike [var](concept:var) before declare returning [undefined](concept:undefined)).

## How it works

```js
console.log(notDeclared); // ReferenceError

let x = 1;
{
  console.log(x); // TDZ ReferenceError
  let x = 2;
}
```

## Common confusion

`typeof undeclaredName` returns `"undefined"` without throwing. Accessing a [TDZ](concept:temporal-dead-zone) [binding](concept:binding) throws even with `typeof` in [some](concept:array-some-every) cases. [Strict mode](concept:strict-mode) does not change [ReferenceError](concept:reference-error) for [TDZ](concept:temporal-dead-zone).

## In practice

Debugging: check spelling, import paths, [block scope](concept:block-scope) leaks, and [hoisting](concept:hoisting) order.
